scope: Second hash-scoped correction of the sole remaining non-accepted dossier; fifteen accepted manifests remain byte-identical.

revisions.boundaryRationale: May isolates the May 6 flash crash. The live CPB July monitor page exposes the official downloadable table that records positive May world-trade growth and positive second-quarter momentum, while the WTO later raised its annual forecast. The label is negative only for cross-border real transmission, not for market functioning.

evidenceItems.provider: CPB Netherlands Bureau for Economic Policy Analysis
evidenceItems.summary: The live CPB release page provides the official July 2010 World Trade Monitor download; its table reports world-trade volume growth of 2.2 percent in May and positive second-quarter growth of 3.4 percent, directly measuring continued cross-border real activity around the flash crash.

WTO economists raised the 2010 world-trade growth forecast to 13.5 percent after faster-than-expected recovery and reported that merchandise trade rose sharply in the first two quarters, confirming that the May market shock did not interrupt the cross-border recovery.

counterEvidence.provider: U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission and Securities and Exchange Commission
counterEvidence.summary: The CFTC and SEC joint investigation identifies the severe and rapid market disruption of May 6, preserving the underlying broad-market event as counterevidence to the mechanism-specific hard negative.
